title: JSONPlaceholder public posts and comments
prompt: Create an MCP server for JSONPlaceholder public testing endpoints. Base URL: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com. Authentication: none. Endpoints: GET /posts with optional userId query parameter; GET /posts/{id}; GET /posts/{id}/comments; GET /comments with optional postId query parameter; GET /users/{id}; POST /posts with harmless fake JSON fields title, body, and userId. JSONPlaceholder write operations are fake and do not persist data.

probes.prompt: Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to list JSONPlaceholder posts. If arguments are needed, use userId=1 or a limit of 3. Return a compact answer with at least one post id or title.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to fetch comments for JSONPlaceholder postId=1 or post id 1. Return a compact answer with at least one email or comment id.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to fetch JSONPlaceholder user id 1. Return the user's name or username.

skipRules.diagnostic: Mutation outside the safe fake POST subset is skipped.

title: HTTPBin public request tools
prompt: Create an MCP server for HTTPBin public endpoints. Base URL: https://httpbin.org. Authentication: none. Endpoints: GET /get with arbitrary query parameters; POST /post with JSON body; GET /anything/{value}; GET /status/{code} with path parameter code. Use only safe public HTTPBin endpoints.

probes.prompt: Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to call HTTPBin GET /get with query parameter benchmark=toolcall. Return the echoed args.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to call HTTPBin /anything/matrix-benchmark. Return the echoed URL or path.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to call HTTPBin POST /post with JSON {"benchmark":"toolcall"}. Return the echoed JSON.

skipRules.diagnostic: HTTP status, redirect, and delay probes are skipped to keep pass-rate semantics stable.

title: Rick and Morty public paginated API
prompt: Create an MCP server for the Rick and Morty API. Base URL: https://rickandmortyapi.com/api. Authentication: none. Endpoints: GET /character with query parameters page, name, status, species, type, and gender; GET /character/{id}; GET /location with page and name; GET /episode/{id}. Responses are JSON and list endpoints include pagination info.

probes.prompt: Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to search Rick and Morty characters with name=rick and page=1 if supported. Return one character name and id.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to list or fetch Rick and Morty locations with page=1 if supported. Return one location name.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to fetch Rick and Morty episode id 1. Return the episode name.

skipRules.diagnostic: The public Rick and Morty API benchmark only probes public GET endpoints.

title: TheDogAPI public GET subset
prompt: Create an MCP server for the public GET subset of TheDogAPI. Base URL: https://api.thedogapi.com/v1. Authentication: none for this benchmark subset. Endpoints: GET /images/search with query parameters limit, size, mime_types, format, has_breeds, order, page; GET /breeds; GET /breeds/search with query parameter q; GET /images/{image_id}. Do not include votes, favourites, uploads, account, or API-key-only operations in benchmark probes.

probes.prompt: Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to search TheDogAPI images with limit=1 and has_breeds=1 if supported. Return the image id or URL.
Use the active MCP tool named "{toolName}" to list TheDogAPI breeds or search breeds with q=hound if supported. Return one breed name.

skipRules.diagnostic: TheDogAPI auth-required or mutation tools are excluded from the live-public benchmark.
